none: Motor vehicle occupant crash protection, Proposed rulemaking : environmental impact statement.

The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration proposed several options to improve motor vehicle occupant safety. The options include continuing current seat belt requirements, encouraging states to enforce seat belt laws, testing passive restraint systems like airbags, mandating passive restraints in all cars, or offering them as an option. The aim is to reduce deaths and injuries from car accidents. Each option has different environmental impacts, such as increased fuel consumption and pollution. The community and environment would be affected by changes in vehicle manufacturing and disposal processes.
Motor vehicle occupant crash protection, Proposed rulemaking : environmental impact statement.

The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration proposed several options to improve motor vehicle occupant safety. The options include continuing current seat belt requirements, encouraging states to enforce seat belt laws, testing passive restraint systems like airbags, mandating passive restraints in all cars, or offering them as an option. The aim is to reduce deaths and injuries from car accidents. Each option has different environmental impacts, such as increased fuel consumption and pollution. The community and environment would be affected by changes in vehicle manufacturing and disposal processes.

The proposed rulemaking by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration aimed to enhance motor vehicle occupant safety in response to the high rates of injuries and fatalities from car accidents. There was some public discussion regarding the potential environmental impacts of the proposed options, particularly concerning increased fuel consumption and pollution from manufacturing changes. Ultimately, the initiative sought to balance safety improvements with environmental considerations, but the specific outcomes of the proposed options varied over time as regulations evolved.
United States. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration
United States. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration
"Over 10,000 lives could be saved and hundreds of thousands of injuries averted each year by implementing Alternative II or Alternative IV." — National Highway Traffic Safety Administration
